text: Fill in the blanks about general physiology.
questions: The cell membrane is composed of a *phospholipid* bilayer with embedded *proteins* (integral and peripheral).
The resting membrane potential of a typical neuron is approximately *-70* millivolts, maintained primarily by the *sodium-potassium* ATPase pump.
Osmosis is the movement of *water* across a semipermeable membrane from a region of *low* solute concentration to high solute concentration.
Active transport requires *ATP/energy* and moves substances *against* their concentration gradient.
The body fluid compartments are: intracellular fluid (approximately *two-thirds* of total body water) and extracellular fluid (approximately *one-third*).

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
